scenario.problem: Average merge time stretched to 8 hours due to massive PRs (400-800 LOC) and purely manual review processes. High defect density reaching production because manual reviewers missed complex logic bugs due to cognitive overload.

implementation.automation: Integrated SonarQube with GitHub Actions. Configured strict Quality Gates (minimum 80% coverage on new code, zero critical vulnerabilities, A-rating maintainability).
implementation.process_change: Enforced a strict maximum PR size policy (Max 400 LOC changed). Larger feature branches were mandated to be broken down.
implementation.human_focus: Eliminated 'nit-picking' during manual reviews; reviewers were trained to trust the linter/SonarQube and focus solely on architecture and business edge-cases.

results.merge_time: Reduced from 8 hours to 4 hours average.
results.coverage: Global test coverage increased from 70% to 85% driven by the Quality Gates.

lessons: Human reviewers should never check for things a machine can check (formatting, missed variables, standard coverage).
Rejecting massive PRs early is better than struggling through a multi-day review cycle that ultimately approves flawed code.
anti_patterns: Overriding failing Quality Gates to 'meet a deadline' immediately translates into un-costed Technical Debt and future production incidents.
